title: Fix 'Payment service not ready' error on payment form submission

requirements.text: Diagnose and fix the 'Payment service not ready. Please wait a moment and try again.' error shown when submitting the payment form. Audit the actor initialization flow in `useActor.ts` and the `useSubmitPayment` mutation in `useQueries.ts`: ensure the anonymous actor is fully initialized and non-null before the mutation is allowed to fire (gate the mutation on `!!actor`). If the actor is not yet ready at submission time, surface a user-friendly loading state (e.g., disable the submit button with a spinner) rather than immediately throwing an error. Add at least 2 automatic retries with exponential back-off in `useSubmitPayment` for transient initialization or network errors so that brief actor startup delays do not surface as immediate failures.

quotes: Payment service not ready. Please wait a moment and try again.

acceptanceCriteria: Submitting the payment form no longer shows 'Payment service not ready. Please wait a moment and try again.' when the actor is initializing.
The submit button is disabled (with a loading indicator) while the actor is not yet ready, preventing premature submission.
The `useSubmitPayment` mutation in `useQueries.ts` is gated with `enabled`/`mutationFn` guard so it does not execute until the actor instance is non-null.
Transient actor or network errors during submission are retried at least 2 times with exponential back-off before surfacing an error to the user.
After the actor is ready, a valid form submission completes successfully and shows the confirmation screen.

constraints: Do not modify files under frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, or frontend/src/main.tsx (immutable paths).
All Motoko logic must remain in backend/main.mo — do not split into multiple actors.
nonGoals: Changing the visual design or theme of the payment form.
Modifying the AdminPage or its data-fetch chain.
Altering the backend canister's submitPayment method signature.
